content:
Key Findings

  • West Lancashire Borough Council recorded £332.9M in total spending across 43,063 transactions from 2016/17 to 2025/26
  • The council's payment types are categorised as Unknown for the entire £332.9M
  • No specific payment methods or types were disclosed in the published spending data

Understanding the Unknown

West Lancashire Borough Council's published spending data reveals a significant gap in transparency regarding payment types. Across the 10-year period from 2016/17 to 2025/26, the council recorded £332.9M in total expenditure. However, all of this spending is categorised as Unknown in terms of payment type. This lack of detail makes it challenging to analyse how the council processes payments or to compare its methods with other local authorities.

The absence of specific payment types in the data means we cannot determine whether the council uses direct debits, bank transfers, purchase cards, or other methods. This information is crucial for understanding the efficiency and security of the council's financial operations. Without this data, it is difficult to assess whether the council is making the most cost-effective payment choices or if there are areas for improvement.

Comparing with Other Councils

While West Lancashire Borough Council's data shows a complete lack of payment type information, other councils in Lancashire and across the UK typically provide more detailed breakdowns. For example, some councils might disclose that a certain percentage of payments are made via BACS, cheque, or other electronic methods. This level of detail allows for better benchmarking and can highlight best practices in financial management.

The council's spending data does not provide any insight into whether payment types vary by department or service area. In many local authorities, different departments may have distinct payment processes. For instance, social care services might use different payment methods compared to waste management or planning services. Understanding these variations can help identify potential inefficiencies or areas where standardisation could improve operations.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to West Lancashire Borough Council asking for a detailed breakdown of payment types used for its £332.9M in expenditure
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to ask questions about the council's payment processes and transparency
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see if any contracts specify payment terms or methods
  • Write to your local councillor expressing concern about the lack of detail in the council's payment type data and requesting more transparency
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and compare West Lancashire's financial transparency with other councils
This article is based on West Lancashire Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
